identity.name: Periodic HTTP C2 Beaconing — NGFW EAL Network Detection (T1071.001)
identity.summary: A compromised host emits a regular, low-jitter cadence of small outbound HTTP requests to a single destination — the canonical Cobalt Strike / Mirai beacon shape. The CortexSim c2_http_beacon EAL plugin reproduces this against the authorised testmynids.org endpoint with a rotating anomalous User-Agent, an optional DGA-style URI query parameter, and an X-Simulation-Run-ID operator header. NGFW E…
identity.description: HTTP beaconing is the most common command-and-control channel because it blends with normal web traffic and traverses egress proxies. Cobalt Strike Malleable C2 profiles let operators tune the sleep/jitter and User-Agent to mimic legitimate clients, while commodity botnets such as Mirai use plain periodic GETs. The detection surface for Cortex is purely network: NGFW App-ID classifies the session as web-browsing, the Enhanced Application Log captures the HTTP method, URI, User-Agent and host, and Network Security Analytics in XSIAM models the regular inter-request interval (low jitter), the low byte volume per request, the rare/anomalous User-Agent, and any high-entropy DGA-style query parameter. This card detects the beacon entirely from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story telemetry: a single internal source repeatedly contacting one external destination at a near-constant interval, with small payloads and an unusual web client signature. A correlation rule fuses the periodicity, User-Agent anomaly and DGA-URI signals into one C2 incident, and the operator filter on the X-Simulation-Run-ID header lets a DC isolate the simulated sessions from production traffic during a POV.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1071.001 — CortexSim c2_http_beacon EAL plugin (periodic HTTP GET with jitter, anomalous User-Agent, DGA query param)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-001 requirements (NGFW inline with EAL forwarding to XSIAM/log collector; egress to testmynids.org permitted).
NGFW forwards Enhanced Application Logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw populated / network_story preset available).

payload.code:
# step-1: Deploy EAL traffic simulator pod and confirm registry
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli list-plugins | jq -e '.total >= 4'
# step-3: Live campaign — periodic beacon to authorised test endpoint
cat > /tmp/c2-beacon-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-001
name: "NDR validation — C2 beacon live"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - testmynids.org
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, c2-beacon, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: c2_http_beacon
    params:
      target_url: http://testmynids.org/uid/index.html
      iterations: 12
      sleep_seconds: 30
      jitter_pct: 25
      method: GET
      dga_query_param: true
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/c2-beacon-live.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Repeated outbound HTTP GET sessions from one source to a single destination at a near-constant ~30s interval (low jitter).
Anomalous / rotating User-Agent string on the outbound HTTP requests.
DGA-style high-entropy query parameter appended to the request URI (q=<random token>).
Operator header X-Simulation-Run-ID present on every simulated HTTP request for POV filtering.

biocs.name: NGFW EAL — outbound HTTP carrying X-Simulation-Run-ID header (operator filter)
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — outbound HTTP carrying X-Simulation-Run-ID header (operator filter)
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app = "web-browsing" or app = "http"
| filter http_req_headers contains "X-Simulation-Run-ID"
| filter action = "allow"
| fields _time, src_ip, dst_ip, dst_host, app, uri, http_user_agent, http_req_headers

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: simulator manifest visible to operator
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "http", "ssl")
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| comp count() as sessions by src_ip, dst_host
| fields src_ip, dst_host, sessions

xql_queries.name: Dry-run: no NGFW EAL fires (safety policy active)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "http")
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| filter http_req_headers contains "X-Simulation-Run-ID"
| comp count() as simulated_sessions by src_ip
| fields src_ip, simulated_sessions

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — periodic HTTP beacon (regular interval, anomalous User-Agent)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "http")
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| filter action = "allow"
| comp count() as beacon_count, count_distinct(http_user_agent) as ua_variants, avg(bytes_sent) as avg_bytes_sent by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_host
| filter beacon_count >= 8
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_host, beacon_count, ua_variants, avg_bytes_sent

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — DGA-style URI parameter on outbound HTTP
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("web-browsing", "http")
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org"
| filter uri contains "q="
| comp count() as dga_uri_count by src_ip, dst_host, uri
| fields src_ip, dst_host, uri, dga_uri_count

correlation_rules.name: C2 HTTP Beacon — Periodic Outbound With Anomalous Client
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(NGFW EAL — outbound HTTP carrying X-Simulation-Run-ID header (operator filter)) OR (XQL(NGFW EAL — periodic HTTP beacon (regular interval, anomalous User-Agent)) AND XQL(NGFW EAL — DGA-style URI parameter on outbound HTTP))

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — Periodic Beaconing / C2 over HTTP
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Rare User-Agent for destination
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — DGA-style domain/URI scoring

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app in ("web-browsing","http") and dst_host = "testmynids.org" | fields _time, src_ip, dst_host, uri, http_user_agent
preset = network_story | filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" | comp count() by src_ip, dst_host
Playbook: enrich destination reputation, block URL/host at NGFW, isolate beaconing source endpoint, open DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: NGFW Enhanced Application Log Validation — HTTP C2 Beacon
use_cases.description: Validate that NGFW EAL + XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect periodic low-jitter HTTP beaconing with anomalous User-Agent and DGA-style URI from a single source.

test_cases.objective: Periodic HTTP beacon to a single destination is surfaced by Network Security Analytics.
preconditions: NGFW inline with EAL forwarding to XSIAM.
Egress to testmynids.org permitted for the simulator pod.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-001 step-03 (live beacon, 12 iterations, 30s sleep, 25% jitter).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW EAL — periodic HTTP beacon (regular interval, anomalous User-Agent)` returns >= 1 row with beacon_count >= 8.
XQL `NGFW EAL — DGA-style URI parameter on outbound HTTP` returns >= 1 row.
Correlation `CR-NDR-0001` raises a single C2 incident for the source.

test_cases.objective: Operator can isolate simulated sessions via the X-Simulation-Run-ID header.
success_criteria: BIOC `NGFW EAL — outbound HTTP carrying X-Simulation-Run-ID header (operator filter)` matches every simulated request.
Dry-run (step-02) produces zero simulated sessions in panw_ngfw_traffic_raw.

preventive_controls: Enforce URL Filtering and Advanced Threat Prevention on egress NGFW policy; block newly-registered and high-entropy/DGA domains.
Require authenticated egress proxy for all outbound web traffic; deny direct internet from server subnets.
Apply App-ID least-privilege egress rules so only sanctioned applications leave the network.
detection_engineering: Baseline per-host destination cardinality and inter-request interval; alert on low-jitter periodic sessions to a single external host.
Score rare User-Agent strings per destination and flag DGA-style high-entropy URI parameters.
Correlate periodicity + User-Agent anomaly + DGA-URI into one C2 incident rather than alerting on each signal.

references.title: Unit42 - Cobalt Strike Malleable C2 Profile Analysis
